nativeRange.habitat: Slow-moving freshwater bodies such as oxbow lakes, ponds, and quiet stream margins in the Great Lakes and Mississippi River watersheds.

summary: A North American native rosette plant with striking leaf-form plasticity — finely dissected, feathery foliage when submerged and broad, lobed leaves when grown emersed. Cold-tolerant and generally undemanding.
careNotes: Rorippa aquatica is a US-native aquatic member of the cabbage family (Brassicaceae) often sold in the trade under the legacy name Armoracia aquatica. Submerged plants generally stay short (around 10 cm) and produce delicate, lace-like dissected leaves; in emersed or low-water conditions the same plant develops broad, coarsely lobed leaves and can reach 60–70 cm when flowering. This plasticity makes it a notable specimen plant but means the leaf form you see at purchase may change as the plant adapts to your tank. Generally advised to keep around 20–25 °C with pH 6.0–7.5 and soft-to-moderately-hard water; it has been reported to tolerate temperatures as low as the single digits Celsius, making it a good fit for unheated and coldwater setups (goldfish tanks, native biotopes). Medium lighting and a nutrient-rich substrate produce the best growth. CO2 is not required but can speed development. Sources disagree on growth rate (Tropica/Wikipedia describe it as very slow-growing while retailers such as Buce Plant and ABC Plants call it fast under high light + CO2) — treat it as variable depending on conditions. Per project conventions, it is positioned here as a medium-light background plant given its potential emersed height; in compact tanks it can also work in the midground. Important: this is NOT the edible watercress (Nasturtium officinale / Rorippa nasturtium-aquaticum); do not consume.
breedingNotes: Propagates via adventitious plantlets (proliferating leaves): broken-off leaves floating at the surface develop tiny shoots and roots that can be replanted. Stem cuttings also root readily. Flowering and seed set only occur in the emersed form.

additional.name: Tropica
additional.notes: Designated primary source returned no dedicated product page for this species (Tropica does not currently list Rorippa aquatica in its catalog), so Buce Plant was promoted to primary. Tropica search URL retained for traceability.

additional.name: Flowgrow Aquatic Plant Database
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature (4–27 °C tolerance, 20–25 °C optimal), pH (6–7.5), kH (2–14), height (10 cm submerged, up to 70 cm emersed), and propagation via adventitious sprouts.

additional.name: ABC Plants
additional.notes: Confirmed temperature 20–25 °C, pH 6.0–7.5, height 10–60 cm, cold-water hardy, beginner difficulty, no CO2 required.

additional.name: Wikipedia
additional.notes: Taxonomy (Brassicaceae, Brassicales), synonyms (Neobeckia aquatica, Armoracia lacustris), native range (eastern and central North America, Great Lakes and Mississippi watersheds), and leaf-form plasticity confirmed.

additional.name: Aquarium Plants Factory
additional.notes: Additional retailer cross-check on care difficulty, lighting, and propagation.
